cot_prefix: Okay, let me work through this question and figure out the correct answer. So the question is asking what the Indians believed according to the letter they sent. First, I'll read the letter again to understand the main points. The letter is written from the perspective of the Six Nations to the officials from Maryland and Virginia. The officials had invited the Indians to send their boys to William and Mary College. In the letter, the Six Nations refuse the offer, explaining that while they appreciate the intention, they believe that Western education might not be suitable for their people.
